libraryPreparationPreflight.agent_guidance: Use the 2.0.0 API rather than the removed 1.x types and methods: RenderingContext and SkinResourceLoader are gone, DefaultSiteRenderer no longer has enableLogging, and SiteRenderingContext no longer has setUsingDefaultTemplate. Initialize SiteRenderingContext with a SiteModel, Locale, templateName, and templateClassLoader; for skin/resource coverage, create a temporary skin JAR containing META-INF/maven/site.vm and any non-META-INF resources to copy.

deterministic_setup.reason: DefaultSiteRenderer is a Plexus/Sisu component with injected Velocity, Doxia, ParserModuleManager, and PlexusContainer fields; upstream 2.0.0 tests use @PlexusTest to obtain a fully initialized SiteRenderer.
The 2.0.0 public skin APIs expose org.apache.maven.artifact.Artifact, but doxia-site-renderer declares maven-artifact as provided, so the generated test scaffold will not receive it transitively.
APT parser components are not part of doxia-core in 2.0.0; the upstream POM adds this module so ParserModuleManager can discover the component descriptor for normal site documents.
XDoc parser components are not part of doxia-core in 2.0.0; the upstream POM adds this module so ParserModuleManager can discover the component descriptor for normal site documents.
FML parser components are not part of doxia-core in 2.0.0; the upstream POM adds this module so ParserModuleManager can discover the component descriptor for FAQ-style site documents.

input_evidence.name: issue
input_evidence.summary: #8134 [Automation] javac compile fails for org.apache.maven.doxia:doxia-site-renderer:2.0.0; label=fails-javac-compile; body_chars=8015

risks: If the generated tests only exercise the transitive xhtml5 parser and protected velocity-context helpers, the extra parser modules may be more than strictly necessary.
Plexus/Sisu/Guice-based initialization is closer to normal use but can add native-image surface area; local Gradle and native-image verification remain authoritative.
libraryPreparationPreflight.summary: Doxia Site Renderer 2.0.0 moved to injected Plexus/Sisu components and external parser/skin artifacts; meaningful rendering coverage needs test-only support dependencies beyond the default scaffold.
